bead.title: Story 16.3: Specs + cross-cutting e2e + raw-transcript-viewed audit even: part 3
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-70a432b5 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 3 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and FEAT-019 specs are missing the documentation sections required to make the Story-16 run-detail page and its evidence browser official. Without spec updates, the run-detail page, trust boundary statement, and raw-transcript audit event are undocumented platform behaviors.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-*.md: missing a Run-detail-page subsection describing the trust boundary ('Anyone with project read access sees raw prompts, responses, stderr, tool args') and evidence browser whitelist (allowed extensions + 64KB cap).
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-*.md: Run resolver contract does not include prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement, or normalized ToolCallEntry.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-*.md: review surface does not point to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- No 'raw transcript viewed' audit event exists in the server or schema.
- Depends on ddx-f04faa86 (16.1 page) and ddx-4cd64068 (16.2 evidence tab).

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-008: add Run-detail-page subsection with trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist (*.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB; no Markdown rendering in evidence tab).
- Update FEAT-010: extend Run resolver contract with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement; document normalized ToolCallEntry as canonical store populated at drain.
- Update FEAT-019: review surface section updated to link to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- Add 'raw transcript viewed' audit event in server (emitted on prompt/response/transcript access).
- Add cross-cutting e2e: full prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence-list end-to-end via Playwright.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend implementation (that is Story 16.1 and 16.2).
- New auth or role-based access control beyond what the trust boundary statement documents.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- p95 dataset (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files) all tabs render and become interactive within one render frame.
- bun run test:e2e green.
- lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
bead.acceptance:
1. p95 dataset (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files) all tabs render and become interactive within one render frame.
2. bun run test:e2e green.
3. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
4. cd cli && go test ./... passes.

{"rationale":"Bead has structural problems: title says 'part 3' covering 'Specs + cross-cutting e2e + raw-transcript-viewed audit event' but ACs only cover p95 perf rendering, which doesn't match the title scope. Description has duplicated PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE/PROPOSED FIX/NON-SCOPE sections (parent context bleed). AC #1 references 'all tabs' and 'render frame' without naming a specific test function or perf harness. No AC verifies the spec updates (FEAT-008/010/019), audit event, or cross-cutting e2e named in title/description. Root cause cites spec files but not file:line. Has parent + deps. Labels include phase/story but missing area:* and kind:*.","score":4,"suggested_fixes":["Reconcile title with ACs — either narrow title to 'p95 perf budget for run-detail tabs' or expand ACs to cover spec updates, audit event, and cross-cutting e2e","Remove duplicated PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE/PROPOSED FIX/NON-SCOPE blocks; keep one coherent set scoped to this child","Name specific Test*/Playwright spec function in AC #1 (e.g., 'tests/e2e/run-detail-perf.spec.ts: renders p95 dataset within frame budget')","Add ACs that verify each title element: spec doc updates land, 'raw transcript viewed' audit event emitted (assert via server test), cross-cutting e2e exists","Add area:* and kind:* labels","Cite spec files with line ranges or section anchors rather than glob paths"],"waivers_applied":[]}

The acceptance criteria only cover the performance slice, leaving it ambiguous whether the spec updates, audit event, and cross-cutting e2e are in scope for this child or assigned to siblings. {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"executable scope is internally consistent: The bead is not safely executable as written because the body contains two conflicting slices. The first PROPOSED FIX assigns specs, resolver-contract docs, a raw-transcript-viewed audit event, and cross-cutting e2e coverage; the later 'Implement only this child acceptance slice' and acceptance criteria assign only a p95 render/interactivity performance target plus test gates. That is not a narrow prose issue: an implementer would have to choose between documentation/audit/e2e scope and performance scope. The description also names dependencies ddx-f04faa86 and ddx-4cd64068 while the metadata dependencies are ddx-868ac26c and ddx-f129bfaf, which makes prerequisite readiness unclear.","fingerprint":"4a4c61c4c495026c402b5fc3d74cf2535cc4223730d9e55d287891592f499c0c","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment"}

{"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"scope coherence between description and ac: Description and acceptance criteria are misaligned. The description claims this child owns FEAT-008/FEAT-010/FEAT-019 spec updates, a new 'raw transcript viewed' audit event in server+schema, and a cross-cutting Playwright e2e — but the acceptance criteria only require a p95 performance check (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files render within one render frame) plus standard test gates. An agent has no way to know whether to implement the full prose scope or only the AC slice. Additionally, the 'one render frame' performance target has no defined measurement harness (what counts as 'interactive', how it is measured, where the dataset is fixtured), making AC #1 not verifiable as written. The description also contains duplicated 'PROBLEM' / 'PROPOSED FIX' / 'NON-SCOPE' headers from a botched decomposition, which compounds the ambiguity about which scope is authoritative.","fingerprint":"4a4c61c4c495026c402b5fc3d74cf2535cc4223730d9e55d287891592f499c0c","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"readiness_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.readiness_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment"}
